articles.title: NZD/USD Price Forecast: Extends rally to near 0.5900 after recovering from 20-day EMA
articles.expand: The NZD/USD pair trades 0.5% higher to near 0.5900 during the European trading session on Tuesday. The Kiwi pair strengthens as the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) outperforms its peers amid risk-on market sentiment.

articles.title: Optimism Weighs on the Greenback
articles.expand: North American participants shrugged off the pessimism seen in Asia Pacific and Europe yesterday after the US-Iran negotiations broke down over the weekend. The optimism was contagious and reports that another round of negotiations are being contemplated during the ceasefire that expires next week. The dollar is broadly weaker. Global equities and ...

articles.expand: ING strategist Francesco Pesole notes that Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey and hawkish MPC members Catherine Mann and Megan Greene speak today, with Greene recently sounding more balanced.

articles.title: EUR/USD: Resilience near pre-war highs – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale strategists note EUR/USD has squeezed through 1.1750 and is approaching its pre-conflict level of 1.18, with support at 1.1673 and resistance at 1.1830.

articles.title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Seems poised to climb further as USD weakens on US-Iran optimism
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair is seen building on the previous day's strong move up of around 125-pips and gaining some follow-through traction on Tuesday.

articles.title: JPY: April hike risk and policy lag – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong highlight that Japanese Yen underperformed despite a softer US Dollar, as muted BoJ communication left it isolated. Markets have pared April BoJ hike expectations, yet OCBC still sees an April rate hike as likely.

articles.title: EUR/GBP Price Forecasts: Treads water at 0.8700, awaiting Bailey and Lagarde
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) keeps trading sideways around 0.8700 against the British Pound GBP) on Tuesday.

articles.title: EUR/CAD treads water above 1.6200 ahead of ECB’s Lagarde speech
articles.expand: EUR/CAD remains flat after registering little losses in the previous day, trading around 1.6220 during the European hours on Tuesday.

articles.title: ECB’s Rehn: Middle East war raises inflation risks, medium-term impact remains uncertain
articles.expand: European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council member Olli Rehn said that the damage inflicted on energy production infrastructure in the Middle East could have lasting consequences, even after the most intense phase of the conflict ends.

articles.title: EUR/USD: Hawkish ECB talk and geopolitics – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Francesco Pesole highlights that ECB President Christine Lagarde and other Governing Council members are expected to maintain a broadly hawkish tone given Gulf volatility. Markets price modest tightening at the 30 April meeting and two further hikes this year.

articles.title: NBP expands gold reserves and moves closer to the global Top 10
articles.expand: The NBP increased its gold reserves in March 2026 by about 13 tons, taking advantage of a sharp price correction. Total holdings rose to around 580 tons. Despite a lower valuation due to falling prices, the central bank continues its strategy of accumulation and is now just over 30 tons away from entering the global top 10 in gold reserves.
